content.full_text:
March 16, 1992

Scott Hallabrin, Acting General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 80
Sacramento, California - 95814

Dear Mr. Hallabrin:

Pursuant to our conversations with FPPC staff, please accept this request for a ruling as to whether a conflict of interest exists for a member of the City of Encinitas' Old Encinitas Community Advisory Board (CAB).

The CAB is a City Council-appointed decision-making body, similar to a planning commission, with jurisdiction over one of five community areas in the City. (The City also has a separate planning commission with City-wide authority over specified types of development applications.) In this case, the CAB is considering the proposed development of a transit station, including commuter rail and intercity bus service, which is proposed to be built and operated by the public transit agency in this area, the North County Transit District (NCTD). The City and NCTD are operating under an agreement which gives the City approval authority over this project, per the City's established site plan review process (called the "design review process"). The CAB is the specified approval body under this agreement.

The CAB is a five-member board. Two of its members have already stepped down from considering this project, due to conflicts. The City has received the allegation that a third CAB member, Sally Cowen, also has a conflict, in that her husband is a realty broker who currently has two sales listings adjacent to the proposed transit station site. (One listing is for sale of a residential condominium dwelling unit in a project which is across the street from the site; the other is for a commercial property adjacent to the site.) Board member Cowen has submitted a hand-written letter which states her position that she feels she can address the proposed project objectively, does not feel she has a conflict, and does not wish to voluntarily step down from considering this project.

The City, on behalf of Board Member Cowen, hereby requests a ruling as to whether she has a conflict of interest in this matter, which would require her to step down from considering this project. Please be aware that the City's Planning Commission would be

scheduled to consider this project if Board Member Cowen does have a conflict, due to the fact that the CAB will not have a quorum to consider this project with three members having stepped down.

Please let us know if there is any other information required for you to consider this request. We ask for your ruling as quickly as possible, since this project is scheduled for consideration within a specified time-frame.

Sincerely,

Craig Bruce Jones
Senior Planner

CBJ/

c/c - file, 90-039-DR
    - Sally Cowen
    - City Attorney

To: Mr. Jones

March 13, 1992

To Whom It May Concern:

I, Dally R. Conner, current member of the Old Encinitas Community Advisory Board, would and could vote objectively on the proposed North County Transit District Rail Station.

I disclosed the fact that Don Roman (Realty Experts) has two listings near the proposed site because it is my duty as an Honest Citizen and member of CAB, but it does not nor do I feel it will hinder my decision in any way.

The future of Encinitas is very important to me.

I am not stepping down voluntarily on this issue.

I want to Vote.

Thank you

Dally R. Conner
330 La Veta
Encinitas, CA 92024

This page consists of blank lined paper, with no handwritten or typed text present. There is a stamp in the upper left corner that reads "MAR 1 1985" and another stamp below it that says "CITY OF EVANSTON."
